foreachItems.Title: Job Safety Alert: Meth Decontaminated Site Precautions
foreachItems.SafetyIdea1: Job Safety Notice – Meth Decontaminated Sites As we’re attending more KO and council properties, some of which may have undergone methamphetamine decontamination, we’ve added a new notice to certain jobs to help keep everyone safe, we'll discuss this more at our next H&S meeting. You’ll now see the following message on applicable jobs: Job Warning Notice: Meth Decontaminated Site 1.Report Immediately – If you notice any odours, even the slightest, or signs of residue or dampness, contact the office immediately. 2.Ventilation Awareness – Be alert to any indications that the property may not have been well ventilated. 3.Protective Equipment – All glaziers must carry H2 respiratory masks in their vans and be prepared to use them as a precautionary measure.
foreachItems.Meeting_x0020_Notes1: "Committee discussed the new Job Safety Notice for meth decontaminated sites. Members agreed on the importance of immediate reporting and proper ventilation. Glaziers to ensure H2 respiratory masks are readily available. Next steps: Lisa to arrange a brief training session on identifying decontamination signs next Tuesday. Further assessment of ventilation equipment is required."
foreachItems.{Name}: Job Safety Alert: Meth Decontaminated Site Precautions
foreachItems.{FilenameWithExtension}: Job Safety Alert: Meth Decontaminated Site Precautions
